query: USMCA joint review July 1 2026 outcome - did US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? Latest news on Trump USMCA withdrawal or renewal decision June-July 2026
answer:
## Summary

**Did the US provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6? — No.**

At the **July 1, 2026** USMCA joint review, the U.S. did **not** invoke Article 34.6 (the six-months' written withdrawal notice provision). Instead:

- **USTR Jamieson Greer announced the U.S. would not renew the USMCA "in its current form,"** blocking a clean 16-year extension (which would have moved the sunset to 2042).
- This non-renewal triggers the **sunset clause (Art. 34.7)**, moving the agreement into a **decade of annual joint reviews (2027–2036)** rather than a withdrawal.
- **The USMCA remains fully in force** — tariff-free treatment for compliant goods continues unchanged. If no extension is agreed by **July 1, 2036**, it expires.
- **US-Mexico negotiations continue** (rounds May 28–29, June 15–17, and week of July 20 in Mexico City), focused on auto rules of origin, steel/aluminum, China-related economic security, and trade deficits.

Note: AskNews credits were exhausted, so I relied on web search and direct article fetches (Brownstein/BHFS, Globe and Mail, Townhall, RVIA, CSIS, Congress.gov CRS), which were highly consistent across sources.

sources.title: United States Blocks 16-year Renewal of USMCA
Inside the Mechanics of the 2026 USMCA Review
USMCA Joint Review: Process and Role of Congress

key_facts: US did NOT provide written notice of withdrawal under Article 34.6 at the July 1, 2026 joint review; the withdrawal clause was explicitly not invoked.
USTR Jamieson Greer announced on July 1, 2026 that the US would not renew the USMCA 'in its current form,' blocking a 16-year extension to 2042.
Non-renewal triggered the sunset clause (Article 34.7) mechanism: a decade of annual joint reviews from 2027 through 2036.
The USMCA remains fully in force; goods trading tariff-free on June 30 continued tariff-free on July 2, and the USMCA-compliant tariff carve-out remains.
If no extension is agreed by July 1, 2036, the USMCA expires/terminates.

Greer statement: 'The United States will continue to engage with Mexico and Canada to address the Agreement's shortcomings and our trade deficits... the Agreement remains in force pending resolution of these issues or until the Agreement's termination.'
US-Mexico negotiation rounds: May 28-29 (round 1), June 15-17 in Washington D.C. (round 2), week of July 20 in Mexico City (round 3).
Negotiations focus on automotive rules of origin, steel and aluminum manufacturing, economic security vs. China, and US trade deficits.
